raw_text:
Yes — you must explicitly bead the crate scaffolding.
Do not hide “create crate + Cargo.toml + workspace wiring” inside bd-17a0 / bd-17b0 / bd-17c0 / bd-18a0. That’s a classic multi-concern trap and it breaks your own rule (“single-concern and executable in isolation”).

Why separate scaffolding beads

Single concern: scaffolding is mechanical but high-blast-radius (workspace + CI + build graph).

Clear rollback: if scaffolding breaks, you revert one bead without touching business logic.

Parallelization: multiple agents can scaffold different crates independently.

Dependency clarity: later beads can assume crates exist and focus on correctness.

What to do

Create prerequisite scaffolding beads (P0) and make the existing foundation beads depend on them.

New scaffolding beads (add as prerequisites)
Platform crates

bd-17s0: Scaffold platform/projections crate + workspace wiring

bd-17s1: Scaffold platform/audit crate + workspace wiring

bd-17s2: Scaffold platform/tenant-registry crate + workspace wiring

Tools crates

bd-17s3: Scaffold tools/projection-rebuild crate + workspace wiring

bd-17s4: Scaffold tools/tenantctl crate + workspace wiring

Phase 18 crates

bd-18s0: Scaffold platform/security crate + workspace wiring

bd-18s1: Scaffold tools/compliance-export crate + workspace wiring

Dependency patch (minimal + correct)

Update dependencies like this:

bd-17a0 depends_on: ["bd-17s0"]

bd-17b0 depends_on: ["bd-17s1"]

bd-17c0 depends_on: ["bd-17s2"]

bd-17a2 depends_on: ["bd-17a0","bd-17s3"] (because tool crate needed)

bd-17c1 / bd-17c2 / bd-17c3 depend_on: ["bd-17c0","bd-17s4"] (tenantctl exists)

bd-18b0 / bd-18b1 / bd-18d0 depend_on: ["bd-18s0", ...existing deps...]

bd-18e0 depends_on: ["bd-18s1","bd-17b0","bd-17c0"]

That keeps the DAG valid and prevents “missing crate” failures mid-implementation.

Scope of a scaffolding bead (what it must include)

Each scaffolding bead should do ONLY this:

Create directory + Cargo.toml

Add src/lib.rs (or src/main.rs for tools)

Add to root workspace Cargo.toml members

Add minimal smoke test or cargo check target

Ensure CI passes (cargo test --workspace)

No business logic. No migrations. No endpoints.

If you want, I’ll output the exact JSON beads for these scaffolding prerequisites plus the dependency edits for the existing 23 beads, in your schema format.
